game_name: Indiana Jones and the Fate of Atlantis

design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore static environments, interact with objects and characters using verb-based commands, solve puzzles, advance narrative through dialogue choices and discovery
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Investigation and resource discovery (finding artifacts and information) versus external threat (Nazi pursuit for Atlantean power)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Indiana Jones on a globe-trotting archaeological adventure to find Atlantis before enemies do; partnering with allies and using wits to outsmart antagonists
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Three distinct narrative paths (Team, Wits, Fists) fundamentally reshape puzzle design, available support, and gameplay approach; player agency in choosing difficulty philosophy at game start rather than difficulty slider; intentional death is possible but signaled clearly (unusual for LucasArts)

difficulty.lives_system: Death possible at recognizable dangerous situations; designed to be avoidable with attentive play
difficulty.checkpointing: Implicit through story progression; appears to save state between major scenes
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through three distinct paths selected at game start: Team Path (partner support, moderate difficulty), Wits Path (complex puzzles, intellectual challenge, no combat), Fists Path (action-focused combat sequences, optional in other modes)

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression gates locations and characters; path selection early in game gates available puzzles and scenarios
replayability_hooks: Three distinct narrative paths with unique cutscenes and puzzles
Different puzzle solutions available depending on selected path
Indy Quotient Points system tracks completion metrics (puzzles solved, obstacles overcome, important objects found)
Multiple locations to explore with branching story consequences

world_building_techniques: Selectable dialogue options with NPCs revealing plot information and world context
Interactive exploration of thematically significant locations (Barnett College, New York City, Algiers souks)
Cutscenes varying by selected path, embedding narrative choices into gameplay
Environmental storytelling through discovered objects (coat with identity card revealing antagonist, magazine documenting past expeditions)
Character-driven narrative through Sophia Hapgood as supporting figure with archaeological and mystical knowledge

faction_indicators: Nazi agents led by Klaus Kerner (methodical, organized pursuit)
Dr. Hans Ubermann (Nazi scientist seeking Atlantean power for warfare applications)
Sophia Hapgood representing archaeological/mystical community opposing weaponization
Indigenous and local populations in souk and international locations
danger_signals: Nazi infiltration of academic institutions (Barnett College breach)
Ransacking of civilian research locations for artifacts
Threat to intellectuals and researchers (targeting Sophia Hapgood)
Intent to militarize supernatural/advanced technology for warfare
environmental_storytelling: Barnett College archives as site of initial conflict and knowledge contested
New York City apartment as private space violated by enemy agents
Algiers souks representing international search and cultural collision
Atlantis mythology as contested historical/mystical resource with geopolitical implications
